headline: Training-free inference techniques (VLA-IAP, DA-PTQ) emerge as parallel scaling track

insights.title: Inference efficiency becomes a first-class axis
insights.body: Two new papers (VLA-IAP, DA-PTQ) deliver meaningful runtime wins without retraining: VLA-IAP prunes visual tokens via interaction-first geometric priors and gets 1.25× speedup at 97.8% LIBERO (matching the unpruned backbone), while DA-PTQ's W4A8 drift-aware quantization of CogACT yields 42.5% memory reduction and 54.8% speedup with only ~2pp SimplerEnv drop. Together they signal that optimization of deployed VLAs is maturing beyond architectural tweaks.

insights.title: Humanoid whole-body VLAs start landing on real robots
insights.body: HEX (BICHR / XJTU / Nankai / PKU) delivers 79.8% seen-scenario avg and 61.8% generalization avg on Tienkung 2.0/3.0 bipedal humanoids at 13.6 Hz with 2.4B params. UniT (XPeng / Tsinghua / HKU) demonstrates 78% Pick&Place / 75% Pouring on IRON-R01-1.11 with just 10% of the baseline's training data via a hybrid discrete-token + flow-matching head. Real-world humanoid results are no longer a lone-outlier category.

insights.title: LIBERO top-5 now has 3 flow-matching entries
insights.body: Top-5 LIBERO as of April 2026: PLD (99.0 residual RL), SimpleVLA-RL (98.8 AR+GRPO), VLA-IAP (97.8 token pruner on π0.5), X-VLA (97.8 flow matching), Fast-WAM (97.6 flow+video DiT). OmniVLA-RL (97.6 Flow-GSPO) ties for #6. Flow-matching action heads now underpin 3/5 top models, confirming the paradigm shift observed since late 2025.
